revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Karatsu, Saga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Taniguchi Kofun | native_name =谷口古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Taniguchi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Karatsu, Saga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Taniguchi Kofun'''|谷口古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[Saga Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211430|title=谷口古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Taniguchi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill 20-40 meters above sea level, which branches off from the 535-meter Mount Tonbo at the eastern end of the Karatsu Plain, at the southeastern edge of Matsuura Bay. The tumulus has an overall length of 77 meters and is orientated to the southwest. The posterior circular portion is 30-meters in diameter, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 24 meters. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. In an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1908, the rear mound was excavated and two stone side-entry pit-style [[burial chamber]]s with chest-shaped stone sarcophagi were discovered. The western stone chamber was badly damaged and was backfilled after the sarcophagus was removed, so the details are unknown, but the eastern stone chamber, which remains intact, measures 2.9 x 1.6 meters and has a unique structure with four [[corbel]] walls and no ceiling stone frame. The chest-shaped stone sarcophagus, which was dug into the floor, has rope-hanging protrusions on the short sides and a stone pillow carved into the top of the bottom stone. [[Grave goods]] include seven [[bronze mirror]]s, eleven stone bracelets, ironware, and jewels, which indicate a strong influence from the [[Kinai region]] of Japan. A separate burial with a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] in the anterior rectangular portion. A fragment of a cylindrical ''haniwa'' found during a 1989 excavation indicates that the tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from [[Hamasaki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Taniguchi Kofun'''|谷口古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211430|title=谷口古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Taniguchi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill 20-40 meters above sea level, which branches off from the 535-meter Mount Tonbo at the eastern end of the Karatsu Plain, at the southeastern edge of Matsuura Bay. The tumulus has an overall length of 77 meters and is orientated to the southwest. The posterior circular portion is 30-meters in diameter, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 24 meters. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. In an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1908, the rear mound was excavated and two stone side-entry pit-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s with chest-shaped stone sarcophagi were discovered. The western stone chamber was badly damaged and was backfilled after the sarcophagus was removed, so the details are unknown, but the eastern stone chamber, which remains intact, measures 2.9 x 1.6 meters and has a unique structure with four [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] walls and no ceiling stone frame. The chest-shaped stone sarcophagus, which was dug into the floor, has rope-hanging protrusions on the short sides and a stone pillow carved into the top of the bottom stone. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include seven [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, eleven stone bracelets, ironware, and jewels, which indicate a strong influence from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan. A separate burial with a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] in the anterior rectangular portion. A fragment of a cylindrical ''haniwa'' found during a 1989 excavation indicates that the tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Karatsu, Saga]]
[[Category:Completion tier 3]]
}}

The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Karatsu, Saga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Taniguchi Kofun | native_name =谷口古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Taniguchi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu, Saga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Taniguchi Kofun'''|谷口古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211430|title=谷口古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Taniguchi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill 20-40 meters above sea level, which branches off from the 535-meter Mount Tonbo at the eastern end of the Karatsu Plain, at the southeastern edge of Matsuura Bay. The tumulus has an overall length of 77 meters and is orientated to the southwest. The posterior circular portion is 30-meters in diameter, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 24 meters. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. In an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1908, the rear mound was excavated and two stone side-entry pit-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s with chest-shaped stone sarcophagi were discovered. The western stone chamber was badly damaged and was backfilled after the sarcophagus was removed, so the details are unknown, but the eastern stone chamber, which remains intact, measures 2.9 x 1.6 meters and has a unique structure with four [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] walls and no ceiling stone frame. The chest-shaped stone sarcophagus, which was dug into the floor, has rope-hanging protrusions on the short sides and a stone pillow carved into the top of the bottom stone. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include seven [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, eleven stone bracelets, ironware, and jewels, which indicate a strong influence from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan. A separate burial with a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] in the anterior rectangular portion. A fragment of a cylindrical ''haniwa'' found during a 1989 excavation indicates that the tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Karatsu, Saga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Taniguchi Kofun | native_name =谷口古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Taniguchi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu, Saga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Taniguchi Kofun'''|谷口古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211430|title=谷口古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Taniguchi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill 20-40 meters above sea level, which branches off from the 535-meter Mount Tonbo at the eastern end of the Karatsu Plain, at the southeastern edge of Matsuura Bay. The tumulus has an overall length of 77 meters and is orientated to the southwest. The posterior circular portion is 30-meters in diameter, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 24 meters. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. In an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1908, the rear mound was excavated and two stone side-entry pit-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s with chest-shaped stone sarcophagi were discovered. The western stone chamber was badly damaged and was backfilled after the sarcophagus was removed, so the details are unknown, but the eastern stone chamber, which remains intact, measures 2.9 x 1.6 meters and has a unique structure with four [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] walls and no ceiling stone frame. The chest-shaped stone sarcophagus, which was dug into the floor, has rope-hanging protrusions on the short sides and a stone pillow carved into the top of the bottom stone. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include seven [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, eleven stone bracelets, ironware, and jewels, which indicate a strong influence from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan. A separate burial with a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] in the anterior rectangular portion. A fragment of a cylindrical ''haniwa'' found during a 1989 excavation indicates that the tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Karatsu, Saga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Taniguchi Kofun | native_name =谷口古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Taniguchi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu, Saga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Taniguchi Kofun'''|谷口古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211430|title=谷口古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Taniguchi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill 20-40 meters above sea level, which branches off from the 535-meter Mount Tonbo at the eastern end of the Karatsu Plain, at the southeastern edge of Matsuura Bay. The tumulus has an overall length of 77 meters and is orientated to the southwest. The posterior circular portion is 30-meters in diameter, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 24 meters. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. In an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1908, the rear mound was excavated and two stone side-entry pit-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s with chest-shaped stone sarcophagi were discovered. The western stone chamber was badly damaged and was backfilled after the sarcophagus was removed, so the details are unknown, but the eastern stone chamber, which remains intact, measures 2.9 x 1.6 meters and has a unique structure with four [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] walls and no ceiling stone frame. The chest-shaped stone sarcophagus, which was dug into the floor, has rope-hanging protrusions on the short sides and a stone pillow carved into the top of the bottom stone. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include seven [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, eleven stone bracelets, ironware, and jewels, which indicate a strong influence from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan. A separate burial with a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] in the anterior rectangular portion. A fragment of a cylindrical ''haniwa'' found during a 1989 excavation indicates that the tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The {{nihongo|'''Taniguchi Kofun'''|谷口古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the {{ill|Hamatama, Saga|en|Hamatama, Saga|WD=Q5644149|lt=Hamatama}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211430|title=谷口古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Taniguchi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill 20-40 meters above sea level, which branches off from the 535-meter Mount Tonbo at the eastern end of the Karatsu Plain, at the southeastern edge of Matsuura Bay. The tumulus has an overall length of 77 meters and is orientated to the southwest. The posterior circular portion is 30-meters in diameter, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 24 meters. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. In an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1908, the rear mound was excavated and two stone side-entry pit-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s with chest-shaped stone sarcophagi were discovered. The western stone chamber was badly damaged and was backfilled after the sarcophagus was removed, so the details are unknown, but the eastern stone chamber, which remains intact, measures 2.9 x 1.6 meters and has a unique structure with four {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|WD=Q12537|lt=Corbel}} walls and no ceiling stone frame. The chest-shaped stone sarcophagus, which was dug into the floor, has rope-hanging protrusions on the short sides and a stone pillow carved into the top of the bottom stone. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include seven {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, eleven stone bracelets, ironware, and jewels, which indicate a strong influence from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan. A separate burial with a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} in the anterior rectangular portion. A fragment of a cylindrical ''haniwa'' found during a 1989 excavation indicates that the tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Hamasaki Station|en|Hamasaki Station|WD=Q5644129|lt=Hamasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q126475056}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Karatsu, Saga]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The {{nihongo|'''Taniguchi Kofun'''|谷口古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the {{ill|Hamatama, Saga|en|Hamatama, Saga|WD=Q5644149|lt=Hamatama|1=Hamatama, Saga}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu|1=Karatsu, Saga}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211430|title=谷口古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Taniguchi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill 20-40 meters above sea level, which branches off from the 535-meter Mount Tonbo at the eastern end of the Karatsu Plain, at the southeastern edge of Matsuura Bay. The tumulus has an overall length of 77 meters and is orientated to the southwest. The posterior circular portion is 30-meters in diameter, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 24 meters. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. In an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1908, the rear mound was excavated and two stone side-entry pit-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s with chest-shaped stone sarcophagi were discovered. The western stone chamber was badly damaged and was backfilled after the sarcophagus was removed, so the details are unknown, but the eastern stone chamber, which remains intact, measures 2.9 x 1.6 meters and has a unique structure with four {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|WD=Q12537|lt=Corbel|1=Corbel}} walls and no ceiling stone frame. The chest-shaped stone sarcophagus, which was dug into the floor, has rope-hanging protrusions on the short sides and a stone pillow carved into the top of the bottom stone. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include seven {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, eleven stone bracelets, ironware, and jewels, which indicate a strong influence from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan. A separate burial with a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} in the anterior rectangular portion. A fragment of a cylindrical ''haniwa'' found during a 1989 excavation indicates that the tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Hamasaki Station|en|Hamasaki Station|WD=Q5644129|lt=Hamasaki Station|1=Hamasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line|1=Chikuhi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taniguchi Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Karatsu, Saga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name = Taniguchi Kofun | native_name =谷口古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Taniguchi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu, Saga|1=Karatsu, Saga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|27|10|N|130|03|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Taniguchi Kofun'''|谷口古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the {{ill|Hamatama, Saga|en|Hamatama, Saga|WD=Q5644149|lt=Hamatama|1=Hamatama, Saga}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu|1=Karatsu, Saga}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211430|title=谷口古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Taniguchi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpō-kōen-fun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill 20-40 meters above sea level, which branches off from the 535-meter Mount Tonbo at the eastern end of the Karatsu Plain, at the southeastern edge of Matsuura Bay. The tumulus has an overall length of 77 meters and is orientated to the southwest. The posterior circular portion is 30-meters in diameter, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 24 meters. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. In an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1908, the rear mound was excavated and two stone side-entry pit-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s with chest-shaped stone sarcophagi were discovered. The western stone chamber was badly damaged and was backfilled after the sarcophagus was removed, so the details are unknown, but the eastern stone chamber, which remains intact, measures 2.9 x 1.6 meters and has a unique structure with four {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|WD=Q12537|lt=Corbel|1=Corbel}} walls and no ceiling stone frame. The chest-shaped stone sarcophagus, which was dug into the floor, has rope-hanging protrusions on the short sides and a stone pillow carved into the top of the bottom stone. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include seven {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, eleven stone bracelets, ironware, and jewels, which indicate a strong influence from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan. A separate burial with a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} in the anterior rectangular portion. A fragment of a cylindrical ''haniwa'' found during a 1989 excavation indicates that the tumulus was built around the end of the 4th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Hamasaki Station|en|Hamasaki Station|WD=Q5644129|lt=Hamasaki Station|1=Hamasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line|1=Chikuhi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
